story.iWant: to extract colors, typography, and visual patterns from brand assets
story.soThat: I can generate a complete theme file

tasks.task: Implement Website CSS/Style Analysis 1.1: Parse WebFetch HTML result for inline styles and style blocks 1.2: Extract color values (hex, rgb, hsl) from CSS properties 1.3: Extract font-family declarations and categorize by usage 1.4: Extract shape-related CSS (border-radius, box-shadow, border styles) 1.5: Identify layout patterns and spacing values

Implement PDF Visual Analysis via Claude Vision 2.1: Analyze PDF content for dominant color palette 2.2: Identify typography styles (font characteristics, weights, hierarchy) 2.3: Detect shape patterns (rounded vs sharp corners, shadow usage) 2.4: Extract visual weight and density signals

Implement Image Visual Analysis via Claude Vision 3.1: Extract dominant color palette from images 3.2: Analyze visual style characteristics (modern, classic, bold, subtle) 3.3: Identify any typography visible in images 3.4: Capture visual mood and design language signals

Implement Brand Personality Inference 4.1: Define personality classification rules (bold, minimal, corporate, playful) 4.2: Map visual signals to personality indicators 4.3: Weight signals from multiple sources 4.4: Generate personality classification with reasoning notes

Implement Multi-Source Conflict Resolution 5.1: Define source priority weighting (website > PDF > images) 5.2: Detect conflicting signals across sources 5.3: Use brand description keywords to resolve ambiguity 5.4: Document reasoning for conflict resolution decisions
Build Extraction Data Structure 6.1: Create structured output format per tech spec 6.2: Aggregate extraction data from all sources 6.3: Store extraction results for Theme Synthesizer (Story 2.3)

Update Setup Workflow Instructions - Phase 2 7.1: Add Phase 2 Brand Analysis steps to instructions.md 7.2: Implement CSS parsing actions for website analysis 7.3: Implement Vision analysis actions for PDF/image processing 7.4: Add personality inference logic 7.5: Add conflict resolution with description context 7.6: Display extraction summary to user

Testing and Validation 8.1: Test website extraction with known brand 8.2: Test PDF extraction with branded document 8.3: Test image extraction with logo/brand imagery 8.4: Test personality inference produces reasonable classification 8.5: Test conflict resolution with mixed source signals 8.6: Verify extraction data structure matches spec

acceptanceCriteria.criterion: Given brand assets have been collected, when extraction runs, then the system identifies: primary, secondary, accent colors; background colors (default, alt); text colors (heading, body)
The system identifies font families (heading, body, mono) and typography scale (hero, h1, h2, body sizes)
The system identifies shape styles (corner radius, shadows, borders) and arrow styles (stroke width, head type)
The system infers brand personality (bold, minimal, corporate, playful) from visual signals
Given conflicting signals from multiple sources, when making decisions, then the system uses the brief description to resolve ambiguity

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Brand Analyzer module: For Website URLs uses WebFetch to analyze HTML/CSS for colors, font declarations. For PDFs/Images uses Claude Vision to extract dominant colors, typography styles, shape characteristics. Infers brand personality from visual weight and color temperature.

doc.title: Epic 2 Technical Specification
doc.section: Data Models and Contracts - Extraction Data Structure
doc.snippet: Extraction data structure includes: source, type (website|pdf|image), colors_found array with hex/usage/confidence, fonts_found array with family/usage/weights, shapes_observed array with element/corner_radius/shadow, personality_signals array with signal/suggests.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: Processing pipeline: Color extraction (primary, secondary, accent, backgrounds), Typography detection (fonts, weights, scales), Shape inference (corner radius, shadows, borders), Brand personality classification (bold, minimal, corporate, playful).

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: Theme JSON schema contains: meta (name, version, created, sources), colors (primary, secondary, accent, background, text), typography (fonts, scale), shapes (boxes, arrows), layouts (template references), personality (classification, notes).

doc.title: Slide Builder PRD
doc.snippet: Theme primitives include: Colors (primary, secondary, accent, backgrounds, text colors, semantic colors), Typography (font families, size scale, weight patterns), Shapes (box styles, arrow styles, line styles), Icons, Layout, Semantic Styles.

doc.title: Slide Builder Epics
doc.snippet: Acceptance criteria: System identifies colors (primary, secondary, accent, backgrounds, text), font families and typography scale, shape styles and arrow styles, infers brand personality, uses brand description to resolve conflicting signals.

doc.title: Story 2.1: Brand Asset Input Collection
doc.snippet: Phase 1 implemented with asset collection steps. WebFetch results and Claude Vision analysis from Phase 1 available for primitive extraction. Phase 2-6 placeholders preserved. Input sources list available for theme.json meta.sources.

file.reason: Primary file to modify - Phase 2 Brand Primitive Extraction section needs full implementation. Currently has placeholder steps. Phase 1 outputs (website_analysis, pdf_analysis, image_analysis, brand_description) are inputs to this phase.
Defines workflow structure with instructions path and state_file location. No changes needed but important context for understanding workflow execution.
Tracks workflow progress. Phase 2 should update phase to "brand-extraction" and last_action appropriately.

node.purpose: Google Slides API integration for export (Epic 7)

dependencies.note: No npm dependencies required for Story 2.2. All operations use Claude Code built-in capabilities: WebFetch for websites, Read tool with Claude Vision for PDFs/images.

constraints.constraint: 100% alignment with BMAD workflow pattern - workflow.yaml + instructions.md structure
System extracts colors, typography, and visual patterns from provided brand assets
All analysis performed locally via Claude; no brand assets uploaded to external services
Theme files and slide content remain local to user's machine
Use Claude Vision for PDF/image analysis, WebFetch + CSS parsing for websites
Source priority weighting for conflicts: website > PDF > images
Personality classification limited to: bold, minimal, corporate, playful
Extraction data must match specified YAML structure for Theme Synthesizer input

interface.name: Phase 1 Output Variables
interface.note: Variables populated by Phase 1 (Story 2.1) - inputs to Phase 2 extraction

interface.name: Extraction Data Structure
interface.signature: source: string type: website | pdf | image colors_found: [{hex, usage, confidence}] fonts_found: [{family, usage, weights}] shapes_observed: [{element, corner_radius, shadow}] personality_signals: [{signal, suggests}]
interface.note: Output structure from Phase 2 - input to Theme Synthesizer (Story 2.3)

interface.name: theme.json Schema
interface.note: Target schema for extracted primitives - generated by Theme Synthesizer from extraction data

tests.standards: Per tech spec Test Strategy: Unit tests verify each extraction type produces expected structure. Integration tests run full extraction from all source types. Edge cases cover single source only, conflicting sources, and missing elements. Manual testing validates accuracy of extracted colors/fonts against known brands.

locations.location: CLI verification of extraction output display
Inspection of structured extraction data variables

ideas.idea: Test color extraction: Run /sb:setup with website URL (e.g., anthropic.com), verify primary/secondary/accent colors extracted match visible brand colors
Test typography extraction: Run /sb:setup with PDF brand guide, verify heading/body font families identified
Test shape extraction: Verify corner radius, shadow patterns detected from website/PDF assets
Test personality inference: Run /sb:setup with bold brand assets, verify "bold" personality classified with reasoning
Test conflict resolution: Provide conflicting website vs image colors, verify description used to resolve ambiguity
Test extraction data structure: Verify output matches YAML schema from tech spec exactly
